android.permission.INTERNET.description: Allows an application to create network sockets.

android.permission.ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE.description: Allows an application to view the status of all networks.

android.permission.MODIFY_AUDIO_SETTINGS.description: Allows application to modify global audio settings, such as volume and routing.

android.permission.RECORD_AUDIO.description: Allows application to access the audio record path.

android.permission.CAMERA.description: Allows application to take pictures and videos with the camera. This allows the application to collect images that the camera is seeing at any time.

android.permission.BLUETOOTH.description: Allows applications to connect to paired bluetooth devices.

android.permission.BLUETOOTH_ADMIN.description: Allows applications to discover and pair bluetooth devices.

android.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATIO.description: Access coarse location sources, such as the mobile network database, to determine an approximate phone location, where available. Malicious applications can use this to determine approximately where you are.

android.permission.ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION.description: Access fine location sources, such as the Global Positioning System on the phone, where available. Malicious applications can use this to determine where you are and may consume additional battery power.

android.permission.BLUETOOTH_SCAN.info: required for discovering and pairing Bluetooth devices.
android.permission.BLUETOOTH_SCAN.description: Required to be able to discover and pair nearby Bluetooth devices.

android.permission.BLUETOOTH_CONNECT.info: necessary for connecting to paired Bluetooth devices.
android.permission.BLUETOOTH_CONNECT.description: Required to be able to connect to paired Bluetooth devices.

android.permission.READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE.description: Allows an application to read from external storage.

android.permission.READ_CONTACTS.description: Allows an application to read all of the contact (address) data stored on your phone. Malicious applications can use this to send your data to other people.

android.permission.USE_BIOMETRIC.info: allows use of device-supported biometric modalities.
android.permission.USE_BIOMETRIC.description: Allows an app to use device supported biometric modalities.

android.permission.USE_FINGERPRINT.description: This constant was deprecated in API level 28. Applications should request USE_BIOMETRIC instead.

android.permission.POST_NOTIFICATIONS.info: allows an app to post notifications.
android.permission.POST_NOTIFICATIONS.description: Allows an app to post notifications

android.permission.WAKE_LOCK.description: Allows an application to prevent the phone from going to sleep.

com.google.android.c2dm.permission.RECEI.description: Allows an application to receive push notifications from cloud.

android.permission.RECEIVE_BOOT_COMPLETE.description: Allows an application to start itself as soon as the system has finished booting. This can make it take longer to start the phone and allow the application to slow down the overall phone by always running.

android.permission.FOREGROUND_SERVICE.info: enables regular apps to use Service.startForeground.
android.permission.FOREGROUND_SERVICE.description: Allows a regular application to use Service.startForeground.

manifest_findings.title: Clear text traffic is Enabled For App[android:usesCleartextTraffic=true]
manifest_findings.description: The app intends to use cleartext network traffic, such as cleartext HTTP, FTP stacks, DownloadManager, and MediaPlayer. The default value for apps that target API level 27 or lower is "true". Apps that target API level 28 or higher default to "false". The key reason for avoiding cleartext traffic is the lack of confidentiality, authenticity, and protections against tampering; a network attacker can eavesdrop on transmitted data and also modify it without being detected.
manifest_findings.name: Clear text traffic is Enabled For App [android:usesCleartextTraffic=true]

metadata.cwe: CWE-532: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File
metadata.description: The App logs information. Sensitive information should never be logged.

metadata.cwe: CWE-312: Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information
metadata.description: Files may contain hardcoded sensitive information like usernames, passwords, keys etc.
This App uses SSL certificate pinning to detect or  prevent MITM attacks in secure communication channel.

metadata.cwe: CWE-276: Incorrect Default Permissions
metadata.description: App creates temp file. Sensitive information should never be written into a temp file.

metadata.cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection')
metadata.description: App uses SQLite Database and execute raw SQL query. Untrusted user input in raw SQL queries can cause SQL Injection. Also sensitive information should be encrypted and written to the database.

metadata.cwe: CWE-330: Use of Insufficiently Random Values
metadata.description: The App uses an insecure Random Number Generator.

metadata.cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm
metadata.description: The App uses ECB mode in Cryptographic encryption algorithm. ECB mode is known to be weak as it results in the same ciphertext for identical blocks of plaintext.

metadata.cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm
metadata.description: MD5 is a weak hash known to have hash collisions.

metadata.cwe: CWE-749: Exposed Dangerous Method or Function
metadata.description: Insecure WebView Implementation. Execution of user controlled code in WebView is a critical Security Hole.

metadata.cwe: CWE-919: Weaknesses in Mobile Applications
metadata.description: Remote WebView debugging is enabled.

metadata.cwe: CWE-276: Incorrect Default Permissions
metadata.description: App can read/write to External Storage. Any App can read data written to External Storage.
This App copies data to clipboard. Sensitive data should not be copied to clipboard as other applications can access it.

metadata.cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm
metadata.description: SHA-1 is a weak hash known to have hash collisions.
This App may have root detection capabilities.

metadata.cwe: CWE-200: Information Exposure
metadata.description: IP Address disclosure

pie.description: The shared object is build with -fPIC flag which enables Position independent code. This makes Return Oriented Programming (ROP) attacks much more difficult to execute reliably.

stack_canary.description: This binary has a stack canary value added to the stack so that it will be overwritten by a stack buffer that overflows the return address. This allows detection of overflows by verifying the integrity of the canary before function return.

android.permission.INTERNET.description: Allows an application to create network sockets.

android.permission.CAMERA.description: Allows application to take pictures and videos with the camera. This allows the application to collect images that the camera is seeing at any time.

android.permission.NFC.description: Allows an application to communicate with Near-Field Communication (NFC) tags, cards and readers.

android.permission.BODY_SENSORS.info: grants access to body sensors, such as heart rate.
android.permission.BODY_SENSORS.description: Allows an application to access data from sensors that the user uses to measure what is happening inside his/her body, such as heart rate.

android.permission.ACTIVITY_RECOGNITION.info: allow application to recognize physical activity
android.permission.ACTIVITY_RECOGNITION.description: Allows an application to recognize physical activity.

android.permission.ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION.description: Access fine location sources, such as the Global Positioning System on the phone, where available. Malicious applications can use this to determine where you are and may consume additional battery power.

android.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATIO.description: Access coarse location sources, such as the mobile network database, to determine an approximate phone location, where available. Malicious applications can use this to determine approximately where you are.

android.permission.VIBRATE.description: Allows the application to control the vibrator.

android.permission.ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE.description: Allows an application to view the status of all networks.

android.permission.WAKE_LOCK.description: Allows an application to prevent the phone from going to sleep.

com.google.android.finsky.permission.BIN.description: A custom permission defined by Google.

com.google.android.c2dm.permission.RECEI.description: Allows an application to receive push notifications from cloud.

android.permission.BLUETOOTH.description: Allows applications to connect to paired bluetooth devices.

android.permission.BLUETOOTH_ADMIN.description: Allows applications to discover and pair bluetooth devices.

android.permission.GET_TASKS.description: Allows application to retrieve information about currently and recently running tasks. May allow malicious applications to discover private information about other applications.

relocation_readonly.description: This shared object has full RELRO enabled. RELRO ensures that the GOT cannot be overwritten in vulnerable ELF binaries. In Full RELRO, the entire GOT (.got and .got.plt both) is marked as read-only.

rpath.description: The binary does not have run-time search path or RPATH set.

runpath.description: The binary does not have RUNPATH set.

fortify.description: The binary does not have any fortified functions. Fortified functions provides buffer overflow checks against glibc's commons insecure functions like strcpy, gets etc. Use the compiler option -D_FORTIFY_SOURCE=2 to fortify functions. This check is not applicable for Dart/Flutter libraries.
